textPlain: This is a very tempting and commonly used strategy in Rust to bypass the borrow checker. I've used it to implement tries/DFAs with great success (though I can't find the code anymore)

> There is a pattern I’ve learned while using Zig which I’ve never seen used in any other language.I've done this in small applications in C (where nodes were already being statically allocated) and/or assembly (hacking on an existing binary).No idea about the effect on speed in general; I was trying to save a few bytes of storage in a place where that mattered.
malloc with NEAR ad FAR pointers (as was used in MSDOS on processors with segmented memory).
